actors.name: Foodprint Nordic
actors.description: Environmental non-profit dedicated to facilitating food system transition by directing funds and resources toward regenerative farming projects in the Nordic region. Launched Top 50 Farmers initiative.
actors.contact_pathway: Connects to agricultural methodologies promoted by Fjallbete
actors.notes: Not a direct collaborator candidate. Structural node financing and amplifying grassroots actors stewarding the biosphere.

intel.value: Top 50 Farmers (T50F) initiative placing regenerative farmers at center of food value chain conversation; connects farmers with investors, markets, and peer-mentoring networks
Astanor, various educational hubs and investors across Europe
Directs funds from investors toward regenerative farming projects
Denmark-based with pan-Nordic scope; elevates Nordic regenerative farmers
Acts as structural node financing grassroots actors; bridges new ecological practices with economic incentives and supply chain transparency

actors.name: Coalition Clean Baltic
actors.description: Politically independent non-profit network uniting 28 environmental organizations across 11 Baltic Sea countries. Won Nordic Council Nature and Environment Prize for model approach to uniting environmental bodies across geopolitical divides.
actors.contact_pathway: Based in Uppsala; geographical overlap with TPI Uppsala
actors.notes: Not a direct collaborator candidate. Simulates biosphere-centric governance body operating alongside and pressuring traditional state actors. Regional meta-organization.

intel.value: Lobbying for ecosystem-based marine management; combating eutrophication, hazardous emissions, overfishing; implementation of EU Nature Restoration Law
28 environmental non-profit organizations across 11 countries, HELCOM (Baltic Marine Environment Protection Commission), EU Marine Strategy Framework Directive bodies
Secretariat in Uppsala, Sweden; unites organizations across all Baltic Sea countries including Nordic nations
Model for biosphere-centric governance across geopolitical divides; classic tragedy-of-the-commons governance case
Transnational network governance uniting 28 organizations across 11 countries for shared marine commons
Network representing over 1.5 million members across 28 organizations in 11 countries
Awarded Nordic Council Nature and Environment Prize

actors.name: BalticWaters
actors.description: Specialized environmental foundation focused on ecological restoration and preservation of the Baltic Sea, bridging scientific theory and practical implementation through research, policy advocacy, and knowledge dissemination.
actors.contact_pathway: Operates alongside WWF Baltic and Coalition Clean Baltic
actors.notes: Not a direct collaborator candidate. Represents dedicated private-sector funding for specific ecological interventions.

intel.value: First-of-its-kind fish research laboratory for studying reproduction and restocking of endangered cold-water fish species in the Baltic
Stockholm-based foundation focused exclusively on Baltic Sea ecology
Essential for scientific monitoring and management of shared marine commons; private-sector funded ecological intervention model

projects.name: Top 50 Farmers (T50F)
projects.description: Platform placing regenerative farmers at center of food value chain, connecting them with investors, markets, and peer-mentoring networks through storytelling to shift cultural perceptions around food production

people.skills: Food systems transition, regenerative agriculture advocacy, nonprofit leadership

search_phrases.phrase: Coalition Clean Baltic transnational marine governance
BalticWaters fish research laboratory Baltic Sea

source_urls.title: Foodprint Nordic
source_urls.description: Nordic regenerative agriculture funding and network organization
